metadata.title: 📚 KSLearn — Complete User Guide & Platform Overview
metadata.description: Everything you need to know about kslearn — the terminal-based educational learning platform. Installation, features, usage tips, and best practices.
metadata.author: kslearn Team

notes.title: What is kslearn?
notes.content:
kslearn is a comprehensive educational learning platform that runs entirely in your terminal. It's built with Python and designed to work on Android (Termux), Linux, macOS, and Windows.

🎯 Core Philosophy:
• Learn anything — math, science, psychology, religion, music, technology, and more
• Study notes first, then test yourself with quizzes
• Review with flashcards for quick memorization
• Follow guided tutorials for step-by-step lessons

📦 What's Included:
• Comprehensive study notes with real-world examples
• Interactive multiple-choice quizzes with instant feedback
• Flashcard mode for quick review sessions
• Step-by-step tutorials for guided learning
• Offline AI knowledge brain for Q&A
• Progress tracking and study streaks
• Bookmarks for saving topics
• Spaced repetition review system
• Achievement system to track milestones

🖥️ How It Works:
• All content is packaged into .ksl files (encrypted, signed)
• The app auto-discovers and loads .ksl files at runtime
• Users cannot edit or forge .ksl files — only study from them
• Content creators use ksl_tool.py to build packages from JSON

⌨️ Quick Start:
• Type 'kslearn' or 'ksl' to launch the interactive menu
• Or use CLI commands: kslearn study, kslearn quiz, etc.
• Type 'kslearn help' to see all available commands
key_points: kslearn is a terminal-based learning platform — no GUI needed
All content comes in encrypted .ksl packages
Study notes first, then take quizzes for best results
Works on Android, Linux, macOS, and Windows
The app is open source, but premium content is encrypted

examples.title: Launching kslearn
examples.explanation: Open your terminal and type the command.

examples.title: Quick help
examples.explanation: From the main menu, press H or ? to see all commands.
examples.code:
kslearn help
# or press H in the interactive menu

notes.title: Installation & Setup
notes.content:
Getting kslearn running on your system is straightforward.

🐧 Linux / macOS:
1. Install Python 3.7+ (usually pre-installed)
2. Clone the repo: git clone https://github.com/kslearn/kslearn
3. cd kslearn
4. pip install -e .
5. Run: kslearn

🤖 Android (Termux):
1. Install Termux from F-Droid (recommended) or Play Store
2. pkg update && pkg upgrade
3. pkg install python git
4. git clone https://github.com/kslearn/kslearn
5. cd kslearn
6. pip install -e .
7. Run: kslearn

🪟 Windows:
1. Install Python 3.7+ from python.org
2. Open Command Prompt or PowerShell
3. Follow the same Linux steps
4. If pip isn't in PATH, use: python -m pip install -e .

✅ Verify Installation:
• Run: kslearn --version
• You should see: kslearn v1.0.0
• Then run: kslearn to open the interactive menu

📁 After Installation:
• Create data/ksl/ directory for content packages
• Drop .ksl files into data/ksl/
• The app auto-discovers and loads them on startup

🔑 If You Have Premium Content:
• Run: kslearn activate
• Enter your purchase key
• Premium .ksl files will decrypt automatically
key_points: Python 3.7+ is required
pip install -e . installs in development mode (auto-updates)
On Termux, use F-Droid version for best compatibility
Create data/ksl/ for .ksl content packages
Run 'kslearn activate' for premium content

examples.title: Full Installation on Termux
examples.code:
pkg update && pkg upgrade
pkg install python git

git clone https://github.com/kslearn/kslearn
cd kslearn
pip install -e .

# Verify
kslearn --version

# Run
kslearn

examples.title: Installing Required Dependencies
examples.code:
# These install automatically with pip install -e .
# But if installing manually:
pip install rich click

# rich — beautiful terminal UI
click — CLI command routing

notes.title: Study Notes — How to Use
notes.content:
Notes are the foundation of learning in kslearn. They provide detailed explanations, real-world examples, and key takeaways.

📚 Accessing Notes:
• From main menu: Select option 1 (Study Notes)
• CLI: kslearn study

📋 Notes Structure:
Each notes category contains multiple topics. Each topic has:
• Content — Detailed explanation with examples
• Key Points — Bulleted summary of important facts
• Examples — Real-world applications with code snippets

📖 Reading Notes:
• Select a topic number to read it full
• Navigation while reading:
  [N] — Next topic
  [P] — Previous topic
  [Q] — Take quiz on this topic
  [A] — Ask AI about this topic
  [B] — Bookmark this topic
  [0] — Back to topics list

💡 Best Practices:
• Read the full content before moving to examples
• Pay attention to key points — they're quiz material
• Use N/P to navigate at your own pace
• Bookmark topics you want to revisit
• Take the quiz immediately after reading to reinforce knowledge
key_points: Notes provide the detailed theory behind each topic
Key points are the most testable facts — focus on them
Examples show real-world application of concepts
Bookmark topics you find difficult for later review
Take quizzes immediately after reading for best retention

examples.title: Navigating Notes
examples.explanation: How to move through topics efficiently.
examples.code:
Main Menu > 1. Study Notes
Select category (e.g., ms_office)

Topic list appears:
  01  Introduction to Microsoft Office  🏠
  02  Getting Started with Word  📝
  03  Word — Formatting Text  ✏️

Type '1' to read topic 1
Then:
  N → next topic
  P → previous topic
  Q → quiz on this topic
  0 → back to topic list

notes.title: Quiz Mode — Test Your Knowledge
notes.content:
Quizzes test your understanding with multiple-choice questions, instant feedback, and score tracking.

📝 Accessing Quizzes:
• From main menu: Select option 2 (Take Quiz)
• CLI: kslearn quiz

🎯 Quiz Structure:
• Each quiz has multiple topics
• Each topic has 5-10 multiple-choice questions
• 4 options per question (numbered 1-4)
• Type 'q' to quit anytime

📊 Scoring System:
• Correct answer: +10 points + streak bonus (+2 per consecutive correct)
• Wrong answer: 0 points, streak resets
• Shows explanation for every answer (correct or wrong)
• Final results show accuracy %, best streak, and performance rating

💡 Quiz Strategies:
• Study notes BEFORE taking the quiz
• Read all 4 options before selecting
• Don't guess — think through each option
• If you score below 60%, review notes and retake
• Use 'R' for random topic if you're unsure where to start
• Quiz progress is saved automatically to your config
key_points: Study notes first for best quiz results
Streaks give bonus points — consecutive correct answers matter
Read ALL options before answering
Score below 60% = review notes and retake
Quiz progress saves automatically

examples.title: Taking a Quiz
examples.code:
Main Menu > 2. Take Quiz

Available quizzes:
  01  Microsoft Office Quiz  🖥️

Select quiz: 1

Select topic:
  01  Microsoft Office — General Basics (10 questions)
  02  Word — Basics and Formatting (10 questions)
  R  🎲 Random Topic
  0  Back

Type topic number or 'R' for random

[1/10]  🔥 Streak: 0
──────────────────────────────
What is the name of Microsoft's
subscription-based Office suite?

  [1] Office 2021
  [2] Microsoft 365
  [3] Office Starter
  [4] Windows Essentials

Your answer: 2

✓ Correct! +10 points
💡 Microsoft 365 is the subscription version...

notes.title: Flashcard Mode — Quick Review
notes.content:
Flashcards are designed for quick memorization. Each card shows a question or term on the front — flip to reveal the answer.

🃏 Accessing Flashcards:
• From main menu: Press F
• Best for: 5-15 minute quick review sessions

📋 How Flashcards Work:
• Front: Question or term to learn
• Back: Answer or definition
• After seeing the answer:
  [1] Know it — Mark as learned, move on
  [2] Still learning — Keep in rotation

💡 When to Use Flashcards:
• During short breaks (5-10 minutes)
• Before an exam for quick review
• After studying notes to reinforce key terms
• As a warm-up before taking quizzes

📊 Flashcard Tips:
• Don't spend too long on each card
• If you don't know it, mark 'Still learning'
• Review 'Still learning' cards until you know them all
• Flashcards complement notes and quizzes — don't use them alone
key_points: Flashcards are for quick memorization, not deep learning
Best used during short breaks or before exams
Mark honestly — 'Still learning' means you need more review
Combine with notes and quizzes for complete learning
5-15 minute sessions are most effective

examples.title: Using Flashcards
examples.code:
Main Menu > Press F

Front:
  What does kslearn stand for?
  [Tap to reveal]

Back:
  KSLearn — a terminal-based educational
  learning platform for studying any subject

[1] ✓ Know it
[2] 🔄 Still learning
[0] Exit

Your choice: 1

Next card...
[3] Know it
[7] Know it
[2] Know it

Session complete: 5/5 known!

notes.title: Tutorial Mode — Guided Lessons
notes.content:
Tutorials are step-by-step guided lessons that build on each other. Perfect for beginners learning a new subject from scratch.

🎓 Accessing Tutorials:
• From main menu: Press U
• Best for: Complete beginners on a topic

📋 Tutorial Structure:
Each tutorial has multiple steps:
• Step title — What you'll learn in this step
• Content — Detailed explanation
• Code/Example — Practical demonstration
• Tip — Pro tip or best practice

💡 How to Use Tutorials:
• Follow steps in order — they build on each other
• Don't skip steps even if you think you know them
• Try the code examples yourself
• Read tips for expert shortcuts and best practices
• Take your time — tutorials are for deep understanding

📊 Tutorials vs Notes:
• Notes = comprehensive reference material (read what you need)
• Tutorials = guided lessons (follow step-by-step)
• Use notes for review, tutorials for learning from scratch
key_points: Tutorials are sequential — don't skip steps
Try the examples yourself as you go
Tips contain expert knowledge and shortcuts
Best for complete beginners on a new subject
Use notes for reference, tutorials for structured learning

examples.title: Following a Tutorial
examples.code:
Main Menu > Press U

Available Tutorials:
  01  Getting Started with kslearn  🚀
  02  Mastering Quiz Mode  📝

Select tutorial: 1

Step 1/5: Launch kslearn
Open your terminal and type the command.
  kslearn
💡 Tip: First run creates config files automatically.

Press Enter for next step...

Step 2/5: Browse Study Notes
...

notes.title: Progress Tracking & Achievements
notes.content:
kslearn tracks your learning journey automatically — quiz scores, study streaks, and milestones.

📊 My Progress (Option 4):
• Total quizzes completed
• Overall accuracy percentage
• Best streak across all quizzes
• Last study session date
• Per-topic breakdown (score, accuracy, date)

🔥 Study Streaks:
• Tracks consecutive days of studying
• Displayed on main menu
• Goal: maintain daily habit
• Shows current streak and all-time best

🏆 Achievements:
• Unlock badges for milestones
• Different rarity levels (Common, Uncommon, Rare, Legendary)
• Auto-checked after quiz sessions
• View all achievements with option V

📅 Spaced Review (Option R):
• Reviews topics you struggled with
• Uses spaced repetition algorithm
• Suggests topics to revisit based on quiz performance
• Helps convert short-term memory to long-term

🃏 Bookmarks (Option 5):
• Save topics you want to revisit
• Press B while reading any topic
• View all bookmarks from main menu
• Great for difficult topics or exam prep
key_points: Progress is tracked automatically after every quiz
Study streaks encourage daily learning habits
Achievements unlock as you hit milestones
Spaced review targets your weak areas
Bookmarks let you save topics for later review

examples.title: Viewing Progress
examples.explanation: What the progress screen looks like.
examples.code:
Main Menu > 4. My Progress

📊 My Learning Progress
─────────────────────────────
  Quizzes Completed:  12
  Total Score:        1,240
  Overall Accuracy:   78.5%
  Best Streak:        8 🔥
  Current Streak:     3 days

📋 Recent Quizzes:
  ms_office/Word Basics     83%  ✅
  ms_office/Excel Basics    72%  ⚠️
  science/Chemistry         91%  🏆

notes.title: AI Chat — Online & Offline Tutor
notes.content:
kslearn includes an AI chat feature that works both online (powered by tgpt) and offline (using the Knowledge Brain).

🤖 Accessing AI Chat:
• From main menu: Select option 3 (AI Chat)
• CLI: kslearn chat

📡 Online Mode (tgpt):
• Requires internet connection
• Powered by AI providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.)
• Configure provider with: kslearn ai-config
• Best for: complex questions, creative explanations

🧠 Offline Mode (Knowledge Brain):
• Works without internet
• Searches pre-loaded Q&A database
• Keyword-based matching
• Fast responses, no API needed
• Configure brain content with .ksl packages

💡 When to Use Each:
• Online: Complex topics, creative explanations, new subjects
• Offline: Quick reference, basic questions, no internet

💬 Chat Tips:
• Ask specific questions for best results
• In offline mode, use keywords the brain recognizes
• Conversations are saved to the Knowledge Brain
• You can add new Q&A pairs through chat interactions
key_points: Online mode needs internet, offline mode doesn't
Configure online AI with: kslearn ai-config
Offline brain uses keyword matching
Ask specific questions for best results
Conversations are saved for future reference

examples.title: Asking the AI a Question
examples.code:
Main Menu > 3. AI Chat

Choose mode:
  [1] Online AI (tgpt)
  [2] Offline Brain

Select: 2 (Offline)

Q: What is the difference between
   Word and Excel?

A: Word is a word processing app for
   creating documents (letters, reports,
   resumes). Excel is a spreadsheet app
   for working with data, formulas,
   and calculations.

Was this helpful? (y/n): y
✓ Feedback saved!

Type 'quit' to exit.

notes.title: Settings & Customization
notes.content:
Customize kslearn to fit your learning style and preferences.

⚙️ Accessing Settings:
• From main menu: Press C
• CLI: kslearn config

🎨 Available Settings:
• Username — Your display name
• Daily Goal — Target minutes of study per day
• Theme — Dark or light terminal theme
• Color Scheme — Choose accent color (cyan, green, blue, etc.)
• Language — Interface language (English default)
• Difficulty — Mixed, beginner, intermediate, advanced
• Show Explanations — Toggle quiz explanations after answers
• Auto Timer — Auto-end study sessions after X minutes
• Notification — Enable/disable study reminders

📝 Editing Config:
• Interactive: kslearn config --edit
• View current: kslearn config
• Reset to defaults: kslearn config --reset

👤 Multiple Profiles:
• Switch between user profiles from main menu (Press D)
• Each profile has separate progress, bookmarks, settings
• Useful for shared devices or different subjects
key_points: Settings are accessible from the main menu (C)
Daily goals help you stay consistent
Multiple profiles support separate users
Reset config with: kslearn config --reset
Color scheme and theme customize the UI

examples.title: Changing Your Username
examples.code:
kslearn config --edit

Current settings:
  username: User
  daily_goal_minutes: 30
  theme: dark
  ...

Edit username:
  username: John

Save? (y/n): y
✓ Settings saved!

examples.title: Switching Profiles
examples.code:
Main Menu > Press D

👤 User Profiles
  [1] Default User (current)
  [2] Add New Profile
  [3] Switch Profile
  [0] Back

Select: 2
Profile name: Sarah
✓ Profile created!

notes.title: Content System — .ksl Packages
notes.content:
All learning content in kslearn comes packaged as .ksl (KSLearn Package) files. These are encrypted, digitally signed packages.

📦 What is a .ksl File?
• Encrypted content package (AES-256)
• Contains notes, quizzes, flashcards, and/or tutorials
• Digitally signed — only the creator can build them
• Users can load and study, but not edit or forge
• Self-describing — the header tells the app what type of content it contains

📁 Where .ksl Files Go:
• Place all .ksl files in: data/ksl/
• The app auto-discovers every .ksl file on startup
• Header is read to determine content type (notes, quiz, flashcard, tutorial, brain)
• Content is decrypted in memory only — never written to disk as plain text

🔑 Activation:
• Free content: works immediately, no key needed
• Premium content: requires activation key
• Run: kslearn activate to enter your key
• Key is saved to: ~/.kslearn/licenses/content.key

🛠️ Creating .ksl Files (For Creators):
• Source content: Create JSON files with notes, quizzes, etc.
• Pack tool: python ksl_tool.py (interactive)
• Output: Saves to data/ksl/ directory
• brain.ksl is separate from content.ksl
• The creator tool encrypts and signs automatically
key_points: .ksl files are encrypted, signed content packages
All .ksl files go in data/ksl/
App auto-discovers and loads them on startup
Decryption happens in memory only
Use python ksl_tool.py to create packages from JSON

examples.title: Adding Content to kslearn
examples.code:
# As a creator:
python ksl_tool.py
  → Type 1 (Combined)
  → Enter package name
  → Enter JSON file paths
  → Saves to data/ksl/content.ksl

# As a user:
# Just copy the .ksl file:
cp content.ksl ~/kslearn/data/ksl/

# If premium, activate:
kslearn activate
  → Enter your key

# Run kslearn — content loads automatically!

notes.title: CLI Commands Reference
notes.content:
Complete reference of all kslearn CLI commands.

📋 Core Commands:
• kslearn — Launch interactive menu
• ksl — Shortcut for kslearn
• kslearn study — Browse learning notes
• kslearn quiz — Browse and start quizzes
• kslearn quiz <topic> — Start specific quiz
• kslearn quiz --random — Random quiz
• kslearn chat — AI chat (online/offline)
• kslearn flashcards — Flashcard mode
• kslearn brain — Knowledge Brain stats

🔧 Configuration:
• kslearn config — View settings
• kslearn config --edit — Edit interactively
• kslearn config --reset — Reset to defaults
• kslearn ai-config — Configure AI provider

📦 Content Management:
• kslearn activate — Activate purchased content
• kslearn activate --key KEY — Activate directly
• kslearn protect — Set master signing key
• kslearn verify — Verify content integrity

📊 Progress & Info:
• kslearn track — View learning tracks
• kslearn daily — Today's recommended lesson
• kslearn --version — Show version
• kslearn --help — Show help

💡 Pro Tips:
• Use 'ksl' as a quick shortcut
• All commands work without the interactive menu
• Combine with shell scripts for automated study sessions
• Use --help on any command for detailed usage
key_points: 'ksl' is the quick shortcut for 'kslearn'
All features accessible via CLI commands
Use --help on any command for detailed usage
Commands work without the interactive menu
Combine with shell scripts for automation

examples.title: Quick Study Session via CLI
examples.explanation: Start studying without the interactive menu.
examples.code:
# Study notes directly
kslearn study

# Start a specific quiz
kslearn quiz ms_office

# Random quiz challenge
kslearn quiz --random

# Quick AI question
kslearn chat

# Check your progress
kslearn config

notes.title: Troubleshooting & FAQ
notes.content:
Common issues and their solutions.

🔧 Common Issues:

1. 'Command not found: kslearn'
   • Run: pip install -e .
   • Make sure ~/.local/bin is in your PATH
   • Or use: python -m kslearn.cli

2. 'No content found' or empty menus
   • Create data/ksl/ directory
   • Add .ksl files to it
   • Or create JSON files and pack with ksl_tool.py

3. 'Decryption failed' on premium content
   • Run: kslearn activate
   • Enter your correct key
   • Check for typos in the key

4. Quiz shows no topics
   • Make sure .ksl files contain 'quiz_topics' key
   • Re-pack JSON with ksl_tool.py

5. AI chat offline mode has no answers
   • The Knowledge Brain needs Q&A data
   • Create a brain.ksl with Q&A pairs
   • Pack with: python ksl_tool.py → Type 2 (Brain)

6. Terminal display looks broken
   • Ensure your terminal supports Unicode
   • Try a different terminal emulator
   • Set TERM=xterm-256color

7. pip install fails on Termux
   • Run: pkg install python
   • Then: pip install --no-cache-dir rich click
   • Then: pip install -e .

💡 General Tips:
• Keep kslearn updated with: git pull
• Back up your ~/.kslearn/ folder (contains keys and config)
• Use 'kslearn verify' to check content integrity
• Report bugs on GitHub
key_points: 'pip install -e .' fixes most installation issues
data/ksl/ must exist with .ksl files for content to load
Activation key must be exact — no typos
Knowledge Brain needs brain.ksl with Q&A data
Back up ~/.kslearn/ to preserve keys and settings

examples.title: Fixing 'No Content Found'
examples.explanation: Most common issue for new users.
examples.code:
# Check if data/ksl exists
ls data/ksl/

# If empty, create it
mkdir -p data/ksl/

# Add .ksl files
cp content.ksl data/ksl/
cp brain.ksl data/ksl/

# Verify
kslearn
# Content should now appear!

examples.title: Fixing AI Offline Mode
examples.explanation: When offline AI has no answers.
examples.code:
# Check if brain.ksl exists
ls data/ksl/brain.ksl

# If missing, create it:
python ksl_tool.py
  → Type 2 (Brain)
  → Enter JSON with brain_qa key
  → Saves to data/ksl/brain.ksl

# Restart kslearn
kslearn
# AI offline mode should now work!

questions.question: What command launches kslearn's interactive menu?
questions.explanation: Simply typing 'kslearn' or the shortcut 'ksl' opens the full interactive menu.

questions.question: Where should .ksl content files be placed?
questions.explanation: All .ksl files go in data/ksl/. The app auto-discovers every .ksl file in this directory.

questions.question: What is the shortcut command for kslearn?
questions.explanation: 'ksl' is the shortcut. It's defined as an entry point alongside 'kslearn' in setup.py.

questions.question: What does kslearn use for its content packages?
questions.explanation: .ksl (KSLearn Package) files are encrypted, signed packages containing notes, quizzes, flashcards, and tutorials.

questions.question: Which menu option opens Study Notes?
questions.explanation: Option 1 (Study Notes) from the main menu opens the notes browser.

questions.question: What happens when you run kslearn for the first time?
options: Nothing — you must configure it first
It creates config files and directories automatically
It downloads content from the internet
questions.explanation: kslearn automatically creates config files and the data directory structure on first run.

questions.question: Which platforms does kslearn support?
questions.explanation: kslearn runs anywhere Python 3.7+ is available — Android (Termux), Linux, macOS, and Windows.

questions.question: What Python libraries does kslearn require?
questions.explanation: Rich provides the beautiful terminal UI (panels, tables, colors). Click handles CLI command routing.

questions.question: How do you activate premium content?
questions.explanation: 'kslearn activate' prompts for your purchase key and saves it to ~/.kslearn/licenses/content.key.

questions.question: What is the minimum Python version for kslearn?
questions.explanation: kslearn requires Python 3.7 or higher. Most modern systems have 3.8+.

questions.question: What is the recommended study order for best results?
questions.explanation: Study notes to understand the material first, then take quizzes to test and reinforce your knowledge.

questions.question: Which feature stores your learning progress?
questions.explanation: 'My Progress' (option 4) tracks quiz scores, accuracy, study streaks, and completed topics.

questions.question: What is the purpose of flashcard mode?
questions.explanation: Flashcards are designed for quick review sessions to help memorize terms, definitions, and key facts.

questions.question: How do you bookmark a topic while reading notes?
questions.explanation: Press 'B' while viewing a topic to save it to your bookmarks for later review.

questions.question: What does the Knowledge Brain do?
options: Stores Q&A pairs you can search offline
questions.explanation: The Knowledge Brain stores pre-loaded Q&A pairs that you can search by keyword, even without internet.

questions.question: What is spaced repetition?
options: Studying the same topic every hour
Reviewing material at increasing intervals over time
questions.explanation: Spaced repetition means reviewing material after 1 day, then 3 days, then 7 days, etc. This is proven to improve long-term memory.

questions.question: What's the difference between Notes and Tutorials?
options: Notes are reference material, tutorials are step-by-step guided lessons
questions.explanation: Notes = comprehensive reference (read what you need). Tutorials = guided step-by-step lessons (follow in order).

questions.question: What does the 'R' key do in the main menu?
questions.explanation: 'R' opens Spaced Review — it suggests topics you struggled with based on quiz performance.

questions.question: How are quiz scores saved?
questions.explanation: Quiz progress is saved automatically to your config file after each quiz completes.

questions.question: What should you do when you score below 60% on a quiz?
options: Move on to the next topic
Review the notes and retake the quiz
questions.explanation: Below 60% means you need more study. Review the notes, focus on key points, then retake.

questions.question: Which command shows all available CLI commands?
questions.explanation: 'kslearn --help' displays all available commands with descriptions.

questions.question: How do you edit kslearn settings?
questions.explanation: 'kslearn config --edit' opens an interactive settings editor.

questions.question: What command resets all settings to defaults?
questions.explanation: 'kslearn config --reset' restores all settings to their default values.

questions.question: How do you configure the AI chat provider?
questions.explanation: 'kslearn ai-config' lets you choose and configure your AI provider (Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.).

questions.question: What does 'kslearn verify' do?
options: Checks content file integrity for tampering
questions.explanation: 'kslearn verify' checks all content files against the signed manifest to detect tampering.

questions.question: Which command starts a random quiz?
questions.explanation: 'kslearn quiz --random' picks a random quiz category and starts a quiz.

questions.question: Where is the activation key stored?
questions.explanation: The key is saved to ~/.kslearn/licenses/content.key (chmod 600 for security).

questions.question: How do you switch between user profiles?
questions.explanation: Press 'D' from the main menu to access the profile management screen.

questions.question: What does 'kslearn track' show?
options: All learning tracks and their systems
questions.explanation: 'kslearn track' displays all learning tracks (Programming, Web Dev, etc.) and their associated quiz systems.

questions.question: Which key opens the help screen from the main menu?
questions.explanation: Press 'H' or '?' from the main menu to see all keyboard shortcuts and CLI commands.

questions.question: What tool creates .ksl content packages?
questions.explanation: ksl_tool.py is the interactive packer tool that creates encrypted, signed .ksl packages from JSON.

questions.question: What key format does the packer use for encryption?
questions.explanation: The key 'kslearn2026' is stored as obfuscated base64 parts (_PARTS) in loader.py and ksl_tool.py.

questions.question: What content types can a .ksl file contain?
options: Notes, quizzes, flashcards, tutorials, or brain Q&A
questions.explanation: A .ksl file can contain any combination: notes, quizzes, flashcards, tutorials. Brain is packaged separately.

questions.question: Can users edit .ksl files after they're created?
options: No, they are encrypted and signed
questions.explanation: .ksl files are encrypted and digitally signed. Only the creator with the key can rebuild them.

questions.question: What happens when you change the encryption key?
options: You must re-pack all .ksl files with the new key
questions.explanation: When you change _PARTS in the code, all existing .ksl files become unreadable. You must re-pack them.

questions.question: What JSON keys does a combined package need?
questions.explanation: A combined package uses: 'notes' for study notes, 'quiz_topics' for quizzes, 'flashcards' for cards, 'tutorials' for guides.

questions.question: Where does ksl_tool.py save output files?
questions.explanation: The packer saves .ksl files to data/ksl/ — the same directory the app reads from.

questions.question: What type option creates a brain.ksl?
questions.explanation: In interactive mode, Type 2 creates a brain.ksl package from JSON with a 'brain_qa' key.

questions.question: What is the magic bytes header of a .ksl file?
questions.explanation: .ksl files start with magic bytes b'KSL\x01' (KSL + version byte 0x01) for format identification.

questions.question: Why is brain.ksl packaged separately?
options: It's optional — online AI is also available
questions.explanation: The brain is separate because it's not essential — users can use online AI instead. Keeps the main package smaller.

flashcards.front: What does kslearn stand for?
flashcards.back: KSLearn — a terminal-based educational learning platform for studying any subject

flashcards.front: What are the 4 study modes in kslearn?
flashcards.back: 📚 Notes (read), 📝 Quizzes (test), 🃏 Flashcards (review), 🎓 Tutorials (guided lessons)

flashcards.front: What is the shortcut command for kslearn?
flashcards.back: ksl (instead of typing the full 'kslearn' command)

flashcards.front: Where do .ksl files go?
flashcards.back: data/ksl/ — the app auto-discovers all .ksl files in this directory on startup

flashcards.front: What is a .ksl file?
flashcards.back: An encrypted, signed content package (AES-256) that holds notes, quizzes, flashcards, or tutorials. Only the creator can build them; users can only load them.

flashcards.front: How do you activate premium content?
flashcards.back: Run: kslearn activate → Enter your purchase key → Key is saved to ~/.kslearn/licenses/content.key

flashcards.front: What command shows all CLI commands?
flashcards.back: kslearn --help

flashcards.front: How do you edit settings?
flashcards.back: kslearn config --edit (or press C in the main menu)

flashcards.front: What does the Knowledge Brain do?
flashcards.back: Stores pre-loaded Q&A pairs that you can search by keyword — works 100% offline

flashcards.front: What is spaced repetition?
flashcards.back: Reviewing material at increasing intervals (1 day, 3 days, 7 days, etc.) to improve long-term memory retention

flashcards.front: What should you do after getting a quiz question wrong?
flashcards.back: Read the explanation carefully, then review the related notes to understand the concept you missed

flashcards.front: What's the difference between Notes and Tutorials?
flashcards.back: Notes = comprehensive reference (read what you need). Tutorials = guided step-by-step lessons (follow in order)

flashcards.front: What tool creates .ksl packages?
flashcards.back: python ksl_tool.py — an interactive tool that encrypts and signs JSON content into .ksl format

flashcards.front: Can users edit .ksl files?
flashcards.back: No — they are encrypted with AES-256 and digitally signed. Only the creator with the key can rebuild them

flashcards.front: Where is your study progress saved?
flashcards.back: In config files — quiz scores, accuracy, streaks, and completed topics are all tracked automatically

flashcards.front: What does 'kslearn verify' do?
flashcards.back: Checks all content files against the signed manifest to detect any unauthorized tampering

flashcards.front: What platforms does kslearn support?
flashcards.back: Termux (Android), Linux, macOS, and Windows — anywhere Python 3.7+ is available

flashcards.front: What key press bookmarks a topic?
flashcards.back: Press B while reading any topic in notes mode

flashcards.front: What does the 'R' key do in the main menu?
flashcards.back: Opens Spaced Review — suggests topics you struggled with based on your quiz performance

flashcards.front: What is the default encryption key?
flashcards.back: kslearn2026 — stored obfuscated as base64 parts in loader.py and ksl_tool.py

flashcards.front: How do you reset all settings?
flashcards.back: kslearn config --reset

flashcards.front: What's the recommended study order?
flashcards.back: 1. Read notes  2. Take quiz  3. Review flashcards  4. Repeat — study notes first for best quiz results

flashcards.front: What Python libraries does kslearn use?
flashcards.back: Rich (beautiful terminal UI) and Click (CLI command routing)

flashcards.front: Why is brain.ksl separate from content.ksl?
flashcards.back: Because it's optional — users can use online AI instead. Keeps the main package smaller and modular

flashcards.front: What key press opens the help screen?
flashcards.back: H or ? from the main menu

steps.title: Install kslearn
steps.content: Get kslearn running on your system. The installation process is the same on all platforms.
steps.code:
git clone https://github.com/kslearn/kslearn
cd kslearn
pip install -e .

# Verify installation
kslearn --version
steps.tip: On Termux, run 'pkg install python git' first. On Windows, make sure Python is in your PATH.

steps.title: Launch the Interactive Menu
steps.content: Open the main menu to see all available features. Use 'ksl' as a quick shortcut.
steps.tip: The first run creates config files and the data/ksl/ directory automatically.

steps.title: Explore the Menu
steps.content:
The main menu shows all features:
  1. Study Notes — Browse and read learning materials
  2. Take Quiz — Test your knowledge
  3. AI Chat — Online or offline tutor
  4. My Progress — Track your learning journey
  5. Bookmarks — Saved topics for review
  6. Knowledge Brain — Offline Q&A database
  7. Data Store — Download additional content
  8. Support kslearn — Credits and contact info
  F. Flashcards — Quick review mode
  T. Timed Quiz — 60-second speed challenge
  G. Global Search — Search all notes
  R. Spaced Review — Review weak areas
  U. Tutorials — Guided lessons
  V. Achievements — View your badges
  E. Export Report — Progress summary
  D. Profiles — Manage users
  C. Settings — Customize your experience
  H. Help — Usage information
  0. Exit
steps.tip: Press H at any time from the main menu to see this list again.

steps.title: Add Content
steps.content:
kslearn needs content to study. Place .ksl files in data/ksl/:

1. If you have .ksl files: Copy them to data/ksl/
2. If you have JSON: Pack them with python ksl_tool.py
3. If you're a creator: Create JSON and pack with the tool
steps.code:
# Copy existing .ksl files
cp content.ksl data/ksl/
cp brain.ksl data/ksl/

# Or create from JSON
python ksl_tool.py
  → Follow the prompts
steps.tip: The app auto-discovers all .ksl files in data/ksl/ on every startup.

steps.title: Start Studying!
steps.content:
With content in place, you're ready to learn:

1. Press 1 to browse Study Notes
2. Select a category and topic
3. Read the content carefully
4. Press Q to take the quiz
5. Check your results in My Progress (4)
6. Review with Flashcards (F) for quick review
steps.tip: Study notes BEFORE taking quizzes. Reading first dramatically improves quiz scores.

steps.title: Browse Quiz Categories
steps.content: From the main menu, select option 2 (Take Quiz). You'll see all available quiz categories loaded from your .ksl files.
steps.code:
Main Menu > 2. Take Quiz

Available quizzes:
  01  KSLearn — Complete User Guide  📚
  02  Microsoft Office Quiz  🖥️
  03  Science Quiz  🔬
steps.tip: No quizzes showing? Make sure you have .ksl files in data/ksl/ that contain quiz content.

steps.title: Select a Topic
steps.content: Each quiz category has multiple topics. Select one by number, or press R for a random topic.
steps.code:
Select quiz: 1

Select Topic:
  01  kslearn — Platform Basics (10 questions)
  02  Study Modes & Features (10 questions)
  03  Commands & Configuration (10 questions)
  04  Content Creation (.ksl) (10 questions)

  R  🎲 Random Topic
  0  Back
steps.tip: Use R when you want a challenge or aren't sure which topic to start with.

steps.title: Answer Questions
steps.content:
For each question:
1. Read the question carefully
2. Review ALL four options
3. Type the number (1-4) of your answer
4. Get instant feedback with explanation

Type 'q' at any time to end the quiz early.
steps.code:
[1/10]  🔥 Streak: 0
────────────────────────────
What command launches kslearn's
interactive menu?

  [1] kslearn
  [2] kslearn start
  [3] run kslearn
  [4] ksl run

Your answer: 1

✓ Correct! +10 points
💡 Simply typing 'kslearn' or the
   shortcut 'ksl' opens the menu.
steps.tip: Don't guess! Read all options carefully. A wrong answer resets your streak.

steps.title: Review Your Results
steps.content:
After the quiz, you'll see:
• Questions answered
• Correct answers count
• Total score
• Accuracy percentage
• Best streak

Performance ratings:
🏆 80%+ = Excellent — you've mastered this topic
👍 60-79% = Good — keep practicing
📚 40-59% = Review the material
💪 Below 40% = Keep studying, try again
steps.code:
📊 Quiz Results
────────────────────────────
  Questions:      10
  Correct:        8
  Score:          96
  Accuracy:       80.0%
  Best Streak:    5 🔥

🏆 Excellent! You've mastered this topic!
steps.tip: Below 60%? Go back and study the notes for this topic, then retake the quiz.

steps.title: Track Your Progress
steps.content:
Quiz results are saved automatically. View your progress:

• Main menu > 4 (My Progress)
• See quiz history, scores, accuracy, and streaks
• Spaced Review (R) suggests topics to revisit
• Export Report (E) generates a summary
steps.code:
Main Menu > 4. My Progress

📊 My Learning Progress
  Quizzes Completed:  12
  Total Score:        1,240
  Overall Accuracy:   78.5%
  Best Streak:        8 🔥
  Current Streak:     3 days
steps.tip: Set a daily study goal in Settings (C) to build consistent learning habits.

steps.title: Prepare Your JSON Content
steps.content:
Create a JSON file with your content. Use the templates from data/kslearn_package.json and data/kslearn_brain.json as references.

Your JSON should include:
• 'metadata' — title, description, icon, etc.
• 'notes' — array of study note topics
• 'quiz_topics' — array of quiz topic objects
• 'flashcards' — array of flashcard objects
• 'tutorials' — array of step-by-step tutorials
• 'brain_qa' — array of Q&A pairs (for brain.ksl)
steps.tip: Use the existing data/kslearn_platform.json as a template — it has every content type filled in.

steps.title: Run the Packer Tool
steps.content: Use ksl_tool.py to convert your JSON into encrypted .ksl packages. The tool is interactive.
steps.code:
python ksl_tool.py

# The tool will ask:
# 1. Type: [1] Combined or [2] Brain
# 2. Package name/title
# 3. JSON file paths (one per line)
# 4. Saves to data/ksl/
steps.tip: Type 1 creates content.ksl (notes+quiz+flashcard+tutorial). Type 2 creates brain.ksl (Q&A only).

steps.title: Verify the Package
steps.content: Check that your .ksl file was created correctly by inspecting its header info.
steps.code:
python ksl_tool.py info data/ksl/content.ksl

# Shows:
# File, Size, Type, Title, Author, Created, Items, Version
steps.tip: The info command reads the header only — no decryption needed. Quick way to verify the package is valid.

steps.title: Test in kslearn
steps.content: Run kslearn and verify your content appears in the menus.
steps.code:
kslearn

# Check:
# 1. Study Notes — your notes category appears
# 2. Take Quiz — your quiz category appears
# 3. Flashcards (F) — your flashcards load
# 4. Tutorials (U) — your tutorials load
steps.tip: If content doesn't appear, check that your JSON keys match the expected format (notes, quiz_topics, flashcards, tutorials).

steps.title: Distribute Your Package
steps.content:
Share your .ksl file with others. They simply copy it to their data/ksl/ directory and kslearn loads it automatically.

For premium content:
1. Encrypt with your master key
2. Users run 'kslearn activate' with the key you give them
3. Content decrypts in memory on their device
steps.code:
# Share the file:
# content.ksl → send to user

# User installs it:
cp content.ksl ~/kslearn/data/ksl/
kslearn activate  # if premium
kslearn  # content loads!
steps.tip: Never share your master key publicly. Give it only to paying customers for premium content.
